summary: Soundboard recording captures a crisp first set with a killer Jack Straw and Truckin' > New Speedway, but the second set drags and the overall show feels mixed.
review: The SBD tape is generally clear with good instrument separation, though a few reviewers note occasional drop‑outs and digi‑noise. Musically the concert shines early – Jack Straw, Truckin' > New Speedway, Let It Grow and Standing on the Moon get repeated praise – but the second set loses steam, with UJB lyrical flubs and a lackluster Way to Go Home dragging the vibe down. Listener sentiment is split, reflecting both fond memories of the outdoor Vermont scene and disappointment in the band’s energy that night.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is uneven (strong first set, weak second set) while the soundboard source is mostly good; the blend of positive and negative reviews leads to a mid‑range rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally crisp and well balanced; several fans call it “great soundboard”. A few mention occasional speaker drop‑outs, digi‑noise from SOTM through US Blues, and brief clipping.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is tight and energetic, highlighted by Jack Straw and a fiery Truckin' transition. The second set fizzles for many listeners – UJB feels sloppy, Corrina/Way to Go Home drag, though some still enjoy the jam on Sugar Mag.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for dynamic lead work and a “phenomenal” tone on Jack Straw; occasional lyric flubs and a tired vocal noted.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince receives mixed feedback – some call his shirt‑off antics funny, others criticize his playing as sloshed or out of sync.
band_member_comments.Drums: No major performance complaints, though a few note the PA speakers cutting out during the set.
